title: Why the Zanj Journied to Rimhurmuz
description: We previously mentioned how Muhammad b. 'Ubaydallah alKurdi and 'Ali b. Aban, the companion of the abominable one, agreed to the reconciliation. It has also been reported that, because of what happened, 'Ali bore rancor in his heart against Muhammad and was looking for a chance to wrong him. Muhammad b. 'Ubaydallah knew of this, and sought ways of evading him. He corresponded with the abominable one's son -the son known as Ankalay-asking him to plead with his father to assign his, that is, Muhammad b.

sources.citation: al-Tabari, The History of al-Tabari (SUNY), vol. 37
